summary: Ukrainian air defenses reported high‑volume interceptions of Shahed and other UAVs, while Russian forces launched guided aerial bombs and an Iskander‑M ballistic missile. Ukrainian special forces confirmed control of Stepnogirsk on the Zaporizhzhia front, and Russian “Southern” troops claimed a bunker kill. Security operations intensified in northern oblasts amid the surge of UAV activity.
key_points: Ukrainian air defence shot down 109 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmass and imitation drones across north, south and east on 21‑22 May (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs over Zaporizhzhia region and Donetsk oblast (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
An Iskander‑M ballistic missile was launched from Rostov oblast, striking Ukrainian territory (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Multiple Shahed drone attacks reported near Kryvyi Rih, south of Zhytomyr, and Kropyvnytskyi (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66852).
Ukrainian special‑operations unit “Artem” secured Stepnogirsk on the Zaporizhzhia front after repelling infiltration attempts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66855).
Russian “Southern” group claimed destruction of a Ukrainian infantry bunker on the Slavyansk direction (Source: @tass_agency/376891).
Ukrainian forces reported 75 UAVs downed on 20 May, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmass and Parody drones (Source: @kpszsu/62328).
SBU announced reinforced security measures in northern oblasts (Chernihiv, Kyiv, Zhytomyr, Volyn, Rivne) to counter espionage and sabotage (Source: @UkraineNow/81360).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defences intercepted a record 109 hostile UAVs on 21‑22 May, while Russian forces escalated with guided aerial bomb strikes in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk and an Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from Rostov. Ukrainian special forces confirmed control of Stepnogirsk, repelling Russian infiltration attempts. Concurrently, Russian “Southern” troops claimed a bunker kill, and Ukrainian security services tightened measures in the north amid the UAV surge.

points: Ukrainian air defence units engaged and destroyed 109 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmass and imitation drones, across multiple fronts on the night of 21‑22 May (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
On 20 May, Ukrainian forces shot down 75 UAVs of similar types, confirming sustained aerial pressure from Russian‑aligned drone swarms (Source: @kpszsu/62328).

Special‑operations detachment “Artem” secured Stepnogirsk on the Zaporizhzhia front after intense urban fighting, eliminating infiltrating Russian groups and stabilising the town (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66855).
Ukrainian intelligence reported numerous Shahed drone trajectories targeting Kryvyi Rih, south of Zhytomyr toward Khmelnytskyi, and Kropyvnytskyi toward Mykolaiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66852).

SBU launched comprehensive security operations in northern oblasts, including checkpoints, building inspections and movement restrictions to counter espionage and sabotage (Source: @UkraineNow/81360).

Ukrainian forces continue to monitor and intercept UAVs approaching from the north of Zaporizhzhia heading toward Nikopol, indicating persistent threat to critical industrial zones (Source: @kpszsu/62330).
Ukrainian air assets and mobile fire groups coordinated with electronic warfare units to counter high‑density UAV attacks across the eastern, northern and southern sectors (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aerial bomb sorties against targets in Zaporizhzhia region and Donetsk oblast, aiming to disrupt Ukrainian defensive positions (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
An Iskander‑M ballistic missile was launched from Russia’s Rostov region, striking Ukrainian territory and demonstrating escalation of long‑range strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
The “Southern” combat group reported destroying a Ukrainian infantry bunker on the Slavyansk axis, claiming a tactical success (Source: @tass_agency/376891).

Russian forces deployed multiple Shahed and other UAVs targeting key Ukrainian cities, including Kryvyi Rih, Zhytomyr, Kropyvnytskyi, and the north of Zaporizhzhia, reflecting a broad aerial pressure campaign (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66852).

Russian units attempted infiltration of Stepnogirsk with small groups, which were repelled by Ukrainian special forces, indicating ongoing attempts to destabilise the Zaporizhzhia front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66855).
Russian propaganda channels highlighted successes against Ukrainian positions, though independent verification remains limited (Source: @tass_agency/376891).
Continued use of UAV swarms from various directions—north, south, east—suggests a coordinated effort to overwhelm Ukrainian air defences (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Ukrainian air defences downed 109 hostile UAVs across the eastern sector on 21‑22 May, indicating intense drone activity over Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Russian guided aerial bomb strikes targeted infrastructure in the Donetsk oblast, aiming to degrade Ukrainian defensive lines (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
Shahed drones were reported heading toward Khmelnytskyi from south of Zhytomyr, threatening logistics corridors in the east‑west axis (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66852).
Ukrainian mobile fire groups and electronic warfare units coordinated to neutralise UAV threats over the eastern front, maintaining airspace integrity (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Russian Iskander‑M missile launch from Rostov potentially impacted eastern Ukrainian territories, though exact strike location remains unconfirmed (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Ukrainian forces reported successful interception of UAVs approaching from the north of Zaporizhzhia toward Nikopol, protecting a key industrial hub (Source: @kpszsu/62330).
Continued UAV incursions from the north of Donetsk suggest Russian attempts to pressure Ukrainian rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/62342).
Ukrainian air defence shot down 109 UAVs across the northern sector, including Shahed and Gerbera types, on the night of 21‑22 May (Source: @kpszsu/62362).

Multiple UAVs were tracked moving from the north of Chernihiv toward Snovsk and from the north of Sumy‑Kharkiv border toward Kryrikivka, indicating coordinated northern incursions (Source: @kpszsu/62328).
SBU implemented reinforced security measures in Chernihiv, Kyiv, Zhytomyr, Volyn and Rivne oblasts, including checkpoints and building inspections (Source: @UkraineNow/81360).
Ukrainian radar detected a Shahed drone approaching Kolomyia within 3‑4 minutes, prompting immediate alert (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66851).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity over the north of Zaporizhzhia heading toward Nikopol, highlighting cross‑regional threat vectors (Source: @kpszsu/62330).
Ukrainian units engaged UAVs near the border of Sumy and Kharkiv, targeting Kirikivka, reflecting ongoing aerial pressure from the north‑east (Source: @kpszsu/62328).
Ukrainian special‑operations activity in Stepnogirsk, while on the Zaporizhzhia front, also impacted northern logistical routes supporting the front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66855).
Russian guided aerial bombs were deployed over the Zaporizhzhia region, targeting southern defensive positions (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
Shahed drones were reported heading from south of Khmelnytskyi toward Kropyvnytskyi and Mykolaiv, threatening southern supply lines (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66852).
Ukrainian forces intercepted UAVs approaching from the south of Khmelnytskyi toward Chernivtsi, indicating pressure on the southwestern axis (Source: @kpszsu/62318).
Ukrainian air defence downed 75 UAVs on 20 May, including southern sector engagements (Source: @kpszsu/62328).
Russian “Southern” combat group claimed a bunker kill on the Slavyansk direction, suggesting active operations in the southern theater (Source: @tass_agency/376891).
UAVs were observed moving from the south of Vinnytsia toward Khmelnytskyi, expanding the southern aerial threat envelope (Source: @kpszsu/62318).
Ukrainian special‑operations secured Stepnogirsk, preventing further Russian infiltration attempts on the southern front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66855).
Ukrainian air defence reported shooting down 109 hostile UAVs of various types on 21‑22 May, the highest count recorded in the conflict to date (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Earlier, on 20 May, 75 UAVs were intercepted, demonstrating sustained high‑intensity drone warfare (Source: @kpszsu/62328).
Russian forces launched an Iskander‑M ballistic missile from Rostov, marking a notable escalation in long‑range strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Russian tactical aviation employed guided aerial bombs over Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, targeting Ukrainian defensive assets (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
Ukrainian mobile fire groups, electronic warfare units and air assets coordinated to neutralise UAV swarms across all fronts (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
UAV trajectories were reported from multiple directions—north, south, east, and west—indicating a coordinated Russian aerial campaign (Source: @kpszsu/62318).
Ukrainian radar alerts warned of imminent Shahed overflight near Kolomyia, prompting rapid defensive response (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66851).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defence downed 109 hostile UAVs across multiple fronts on 21‑22 May, marking the highest recorded interception count (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs over Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, targeting Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
An Iskander‑M ballistic missile was launched from Russia’s Rostov region, striking Ukrainian territory and escalating long‑range strike use (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Ukrainian special‑operations unit “Artem” secured Stepnogirsk on the Zaporizhzhia front after repelling Russian infiltration attempts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66855).
Russian “Southern” combat group claimed destruction of a Ukrainian infantry bunker on the Slavyansk axis (Source: @tass_agency/376891).
Shahed drone attacks were reported near Kryvyi Rih, south of Zhytomyr toward Khmelnytskyi, and Kropyvnytskyi toward Mykolaiv, indicating widespread UAV pressure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66852).
SBU announced reinforced security measures in northern oblasts to counter espionage and sabotage amid increased UAV activity (Source: @UkraineNow/81360).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying a multi‑vector aerial campaign, combining high‑volume Shahed drone swarms, guided aerial bomb strikes, and the deployment of an Iskander‑M ballistic missile. Ukrainian air defences have demonstrated resilience, achieving record interceptions, yet the sheer scale of attacks strains resources. Ground operations show continued Russian attempts at infiltration, as seen in Stepnogirsk, while Ukrainian special forces are actively countering these moves, maintaining control of key towns.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is bolstering air defence assets and integrating electronic warfare across all fronts, while the SBU tightens internal security in northern regions to mitigate sabotage risks. Internationally, NATO has warned of severe retaliation to any nuclear escalation, underscoring the broader strategic stakes. Russian propaganda continues to claim tactical successes, but on‑ground realities suggest a contested and fluid front line.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch points indicates coordinated launches from Russian‑held territories and occupied oblasts, requiring continuous radar updates. The Iskander‑M launch suggests possible preparation for further ballistic strikes; tracking launch sites will be critical. Ukrainian special‑operations successes in Stepnogirsk provide a template for urban counter‑infiltration tactics that could be replicated on other contested towns.
